---
title: "Title Goes Here"
author:
  - ${AUTHOR_NAME}:
      email: ${AUTHOR_EMAIL}
      institute: [${AUTHOR_INSTITUTE}]
      correspondence: true
institute:
  - ${AUTHOR_INSTITUTE}: ${AUTHOR_INSTITUTE_FULL}
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output:
  bookdown::pdf_document2:
    fig_caption: yes
    number_sections: true
    toc: false
    keep_tex: true
bibliography: references.bib
csl: "../templates/statistics-in-medicine.csl"
knit: (function(input, ...) { d <- dirname(input); while (!file.exists(file.path(d, 'tools', 'stamp-render.R')) && d != dirname(d)) d <- dirname(d); source(file.path(d, 'tools', 'stamp-render.R'))$value(input) })
abstract: |
  Text of abstract
keywords: |
  keyword 1; keyword 2; keyword 3
highlights: |
  These are the highlights.
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{setspace}
  - \setstretch{1.1}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.path = "../figures/",
  dpi = 300
)
# Load this compendium's package to use its analysis functions. It must be
# available in the rendering environment: install it in the image, or load it
# from source from the project root during development with
# pkgload::load_all(). Left commented so the scaffold renders before the
# package has any functions.
# library(${PKG_NAME})
library(rmarkdown)
library(knitr)
```

# Introduction

Here is the text of your introduction. This compendium follows established
principles of computational reproducibility in research [@marwick2017].

# Materials and methods

Here are the methods.

# Results

Here are the results.

# Discussion

Discussion text goes here.

# Conclusion

Conclusions text goes here.

# References
